generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SteeringServoMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Precise control of mechanical steering components via ROS2 network for ACC functionality"
]

instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Enables precise motor control for vehicle speed management via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationCommandData
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor mounted at specific spatial coordinates providing spatial mapping and depth perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing spatial mapping and depth perception for object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthImageData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects discrete sensor readings for motor, steering, servo actuation, and overall vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance PointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance DepthImageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderElectronicSpeedController
]

// C5: Data Processing Functional Entities
instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Performs DBSCAN-based clustering and tracking for point cloud data processing"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Ensures message type alignment, unit conversions, and serialization for all data streams"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance ActuationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Processes actuation data for execution on physical vehicle via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Aggregates vehicle state and actuation data for visualization and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, object tracks, projected driving paths) and historical data reporting"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, HistoricalDataReportingInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSavingAction, AnalyticsGenerationAction
    DTDFVocab:enables WiFiUDPNetwork
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delegates ACC-specific computations (lead-vehicle detection, speed calculation) from on-board ECUs to cloud/DT infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Middleware
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Full control over ACC operations remotely including enable/disable, speed setting, and emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Middleware
]

// C7: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of vehicle state data including speed, object tracks, and projected driving paths"
]

instance HistoricalDataReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generates historical data reports for vehicle state analysis and diagnostics"
]

instance DataSavingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Allows saving of monitored data for later analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AnalyticsGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates analytics from collected data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides insights on performance optimization through offloaded computations"
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables/disables ACC system remotely with low-latency response"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers emergency braking commands remotely with low-latency execution"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C8: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Represents vehicle geometry and spatial relationships for ACC simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingFE
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulates vehicle behavior including ACC-specific algorithms and responses"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance PredictivePathModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Generates projected driving paths based on current vehicle state and sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance VehicleStateDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data including speed, position, and acceleration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance ObjectTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks generated from point cloud data processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

// C9: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides real-time communication infrastructure between physical and digital components via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPreProcessingFE, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE, PresentationFE
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance WiFiUDPNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables low-latency data transfer between digital twin and external cloud systems via Wi-Fi UDP"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stores vehicle state data, reports, and historical logs for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataSavingAction
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Used for simulation and validation of ACC algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BehavioralModel
]

instance OPCUAProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Potential implementation for standardized data exchange and control protocols"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

// C10: Entity Structure
instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:contains UserInterfaceFeatureElement
]

instance UserInterfaceFeatureElement : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Consolidates user-facing components including 3D visualization and control panel"
    DTDFVocab:contains VisualizationComponent, ControlPanel
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:contains DataCollectionEntity, DataManagementEntity
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:contains DataPreProcessingComponent, ActuationComponent
]

instance DataCollectionEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages data collection and device control for OMEs"
    DTDFVocab:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance DataManagementEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles data aggregation, correlation, and synchronization between physical and digital representations"
    DTDFVocab:contains DataPreProcessingFE, DataTranslationFE, PresentationFE
]

instance DataPreProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Performs initial data processing including clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:contains DataPreProcessingFE
]

instance ActuationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Executes actuation commands on physical vehicle via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:contains ActuationFE
]

instance VisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Provides 3D cockpit visualization of vehicle state and projected paths"
    DTDFVocab:contains PredictivePathModel
]

instance ControlPanel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface for remote management including ACC control and emergency actions"
    DTDFVocab:contains RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** enables closed-loop control via the DTS’s **Actuation FE**, which translates digital commands into physical actions. Key components include:
- **Command Types**: The system supports **Automatic Cruise Control (ACC) enable/disable** and **emergency braking** commands, where the digital twin sends desired speed or braking inputs.
- **Control Message Formats**: The **Actuation FE** converts desired speed into **motor voltage commands** (e.g., for throttle/brake actuation) and sends them back to the vehicle.
- **Feedback Systems**: The physical vehicle’s motor/servo states (e.g., motor voltage, steering angle) are relayed back to the digital twin via the **Data Collection FE** and **Data Translation FE** for validation and error correction.
- **No explicit conflict resolution or authentication mechanisms** are described in the documents, but the architecture implies a **direct command execution pipeline** with no intermediate validation layers beyond the Actuation FE.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components to enable **computation offloading**. The system mandates **sub-100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** for safety-critical vehicular applications, driven by hardware limitations. This ensures real-time alignment for critical operations, including data processing, actuation, and sensor-device coordination. The temporal granularity is optimized for low-latency applications where precise timing (e.g., <100 ms) is essential for maintaining synchronization between the physical and digital entities, particularly in automotive contexts requiring deterministic performance."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) adopts an **entity-centric architecture** based on ISO 23247’s Functional Entity (FE) framework, mapped to three primary entities:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Closest to ISO 23247’s **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, but expanded to host **general automotive functionalities** (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) alongside resource-specific tasks. Deployed on physical hardware (sensors/actuators/compute), it manages **device communication, actuation, and pre-processing** while coordinating with the Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Aggregates **data management and control logic**, including cross-entity orchestration, but does **not** align with ISO 23247’s Operations Management Entity (OME). Instead, it serves as a **centralized control plane** for real-time decisions, interfacing with the Physical Twin Entity for low-latency feedback loops.
  - **User Entity**: Hosts the **split User Interface FE** (3D Visualization + Control Panel) as a unified component, ensuring seamless user interaction. All entities are **decomposed into ROS2 packages**, enabling modular deployment and interoperability across distributed hardware.

  **Orchestration**: Components communicate via **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model**, with **Wi-Fi-based inter-entity communication** (implied by distributed deployment). The system lacks explicit mention of a **centralized orchestrator**, but the entity-based design implies **hierarchical coordination** where the Digital Twin Entity acts as the primary arbiter for high-level decisions, while the Physical Twin Entity handles low-level resource management. The **100 ms synchronization constraint** ensures deterministic timing for all inter-entity interactions."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard defines a **development framework** for Digital Twins (DTs) in manufacturing, structured as a **reference architecture (RA)** without explicit lifecycle stages. The framework organizes DTs into **entity-based components**:
  - **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**: Physical entities (e.g., equipment, processes) with observable presence/operations.
  - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data acquisition from OMEs and controls physical operations via **Functional Entities (FEs)** (e.g., OPC Unified Architecture for interoperability).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: A software representation of the OME, implemented via FEs (e.g., data transformation, actuation).
  - **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for monitoring (e.g., data visualization) or remote management (e.g., OME control). The standard does **not** prescribe lifecycle phases (e.g., design/deployment/operational) or transition protocols, focusing instead on **entity composition** and **information exchange** (Part 2 of ISO 23247). Governance is implicit through modular entity governance rather than formalized stages."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is defined as a **real-time and high-fidelity computational reflection** of physical processes, adhering to ISO 23247’s principles. Validation methods are implicitly grounded in the ISO framework’s entity-based validation, though no specific techniques (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations, cross-validation) or uncertainty quantification (e.g., Bayesian methods) are detailed. Quality assurance processes are not explicitly described beyond the standard’s general requirements."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Internal SW Communication:** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for inter-component messaging.
    - **Inter-Entity Communication:** Wi-Fi UDP for cross-entity (physical/digital) data transfer.

  - **Network Topology and Architecture:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Likely a local/edge network (on-vehicle or onboard computer) for low-latency SW interactions.
    - **Wi-Fi Network:** UDP over Wi-Fi for inter-entity communication (e.g., between onboard systems and cloud/edge services).
    - **Hybrid Architecture:** Separation between SW components (ROS2) and physical entities (Wi-Fi).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **LiDAR Data:** High bandwidth (~10–100 MB/s for 3D point clouds) with **low latency (<100 ms)** for real-time visualization.
    - **Vehicle State Data:** Lower bandwidth (~KB/s) with **sub-10 ms latency** for control feedback.
    - **Not in Document:** No explicit specs on peak throughput or QoS policies.

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2:** Built-in reliability features (e.g., retries, acknowledgments for critical messages).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** No redundancy mentioned; assumes network stability (e.g., mesh networks or failover).
    - **Not in Document:** No details on redundancy for critical paths (e.g., LiDAR-to-visualization) or failover strategies.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation:** Ensures message integrity (serialization/alignment) but **no encryption** mentioned.
    - **Wi-Fi:** UDP lacks built-in encryption; **no TLS/SSL** or authentication for inter-entity traffic.
    - **Not in Document:** No mention of access controls, intrusion detection, or end-to-end security.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** is characterized by:
- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Onboard**: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer (for real-time processing) and Jetson NX (for LiDAR clustering).
  - **External**: Storage devices (e.g., external to the vehicle) for archiving ego vehicle state data.
- **Computational Requirements**:
  - Onboard: Dedicated hardware (e.g., Jetson Orin Nano) for low-latency processing.
  - Offboard: Cloud/edge compute for non-critical tasks (e.g., data storage, reporting).
- **Scalability**: The system supports **dynamic compute allocation** (e.g., shifting workloads to cloud/edge) to handle varying data volumes.
- **Deployment Models**: Implicitly supports **on-premise/edge deployment** with minimal cloud dependency (external storage is external to the vehicle).
- **Availability/Reliability**: No explicit uptime guarantees (e.g., 99.9%) are mentioned, but the architecture prioritizes **real-time processing** with minimal downtime for onboard components.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The **standardization** for the Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 23247 (2021)**, a framework for manufacturing DTs, with the following compliance details:
- **Industry Standards**: Core compliance with **ISO 23247 (Part 2: Reference Architecture)**, defining an **entity-based RA** with OMEs, Data Collection Entities, Digital Twin Entities, and User Entities.
- **Specifications**: Uses **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for interoperability between FEs and OMEs, ensuring data exchange standards.
- **Certification Requirements**: Not explicitly detailed; however, compliance with OPC UA may imply certification via OPC Foundation standards.
- **Compliance Frameworks**: The DT’s modular architecture aligns with **ISO 16340 (Digital Twin Framework)** principles, though ISO 23247 is the primary reference.
- **Interoperability Standards**: Adopts **OPC UA** for cross-entity communication, enabling seamless integration of FEs and OMEs.
The **public demonstration** (Document 1) references an **automotive DT** (GitHub repo: [McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin)), suggesting adherence to ISO 23247 and OPC UA.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** adheres to **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)**, which specifies technical requirements for **latency constraints** (≤100 ms for critical data exchange) and **performance benchmarks** for adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems. While the system does not explicitly reference **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles)**, the context implies **safety-critical operational considerations** tied to real-time communication protocols (e.g., V2X messaging standards) to ensure fail-safe behavior in autonomous driving scenarios.

No explicit mention of **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001) is provided in the documents. The provided references only confirm:
- **Network latency constraints** for v2X services (≤100 ms).
- **ISO 15622:2018** compliance for **adaptive cruise control performance** (e.g., deceleration/acceleration accuracy, obstacle detection thresholds).
The absence of detailed security frameworks (e.g., encryption standards, role-based access) means these aspects remain **not documented** in the provided context."
]
